message:
chore(beads): sync close_reason metadata on br-qc3d7 after gossip payload cap landed

Routine one-line beads metadata sync on `flywheel_connectors-
qc3d7 — [REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-mesh: dispatch_gossip_payload parses
unbounded JSON before gossip caps`. The status was already
flipped to `"closed"` in the earlier close commit (`chore(beads):
close 4 audit beads resolved by this REVIEW sweep`, commit
d4c96011); this commit updates the close_reason / closed_at
metadata fields with the final landing commit hash from the
gossip cap fix (commit 0f531341 — `fix(fcp-mesh): reject
oversized gossip payloads before serde_json::from_slice`). Line
count unchanged (1 line modified, 0 insertions, 0 deletions).

No issue bodies changed; no new beads filed.

files.patch:
@@ -1983,7 +1983,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-q1d0x","title":"[FCP3/P2.2] Carve fcp-policy as the single owner of zone, capability, and provenance semantics","description":"Background: zone binding, capability checks, provenance, and admission logic are central platform semantics, but today they are still partly smeared between shared core code and host owned orchestration paths. That makes policy too easy to bypass or reinterpret.

Scope: define and extract fcp-policy as the owner of security and admission semantics: zone rules, capability scoping, provenance constraints, placement eligibility inputs, and any policy relevant decision vocabulary that should be shared across host, mesh, and CLI explanation flows. Include canonical policy decision records, denial reasons, and deterministic policy fixtures so later negative tests, placement checks, and CLI explanation flows can prove semantics mechanically instead of recreating them ad hoc.

Acceptance: policy concepts have a single owner, downstream code consumes those policy contracts rather than embedding parallel policy logic, and policy outcomes are explicit enough for negative tests and operator explanations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:04.398443Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-28T06:24:43.952352Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T06:24:43.952119Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh-first","phase-2","policy","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q1d0x","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6huk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:21.133113Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q1d0x","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-xn3h3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:21.400710Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2073,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q1d0x","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak: Created fcp-policy crate (commit 2493cbce). Phase 1 re-exports: 60+ types across 8 domains (zone, capabilities, identity, provenance, approval, policy engine, diffs, resources). 7 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:53:26Z"},{"id":2076,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q1d0x","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-28: Created crates/fcp-policy/ with 151-line facade re-exporting policy types from fcp-core. Covers: CapabilityToken/Id/Grant/Verifier, ZoneId/ZoneDefinition/ZonePolicy, PolicyEngine/PolicyDecision/PolicyBundle, Provenance/TaintLevel/IntegrityLevel, ApprovalToken, RoleGraph, Secret management, PostureRequirements, Supply chain verification. Tests verify type accessibility.","created_at":"2026-03-28T06:24:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","title":"[FCP3/P3] Realign FCPC and FCPS wire and durable truth around mesh native semantics","description":"Background: V3 distinguishes a live control and evidence plane, FCPC, from a durable object plane, FCPS. Today the implementation still leans too heavily on host local runtime state and adapter specific behavior, especially around admin and control truth and durable evidence objects.

Scope: make FCPC and FCPS canonical, define durable schemas for the facts the system must remember beyond a single host process, and align store, manifest, registry, and conformance work with that model. The phase must make wire contracts and durable object shapes replayable and inspectable through deterministic fixtures, serialization round trips, repair scenarios, cross crate integration tests, and structured diagnostic artifacts so later user facing tooling can explain what is live, what is durable, and how the two relate. This phase is successful when durable truth can be replayed, repaired, and explained without treating the host process as the sole authority.

Acceptance: FCPC and FCPS semantics are backed by stable schemas, proof fixtures, and detailed transcripts or logs that later conformance, operator, and e2e beads can reuse directly instead of reconstructing from host local behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:01.411236Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:08:59.715602Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T14:08:59.386420Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh-first","phase-3","protocol"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:12.499616Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:12.117871Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ndoro","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:12.308760Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:12.698077Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-vqfld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:11.921808Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2050,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"HTTP admin, local process memory, and ad hoc host structs are adapters in this phase, not the semantic model. If a fact matters for readiness, failover, or audit, it should have a canonical FCPC or FCPS form rather than living only in host local code paths.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:01Z"},{"id":2367,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","author":"jemanuel","text":"All 5 children closed (P3.1-P3.5). FCPC/FCPS wire and durable truth fully realigned around mesh-native semantics. 921 conformance vectors, 13 FCPS durability boundary tests, comprehensive store/repair/GC coverage.","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:08:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-q6huk","title":"[FCP3/P1.2] Publish canonical owner map and forbidden overlaps","description":"Background: the V3 architecture only becomes actionable when every major semantic noun has one clear home. Without that, crate splits and protocol changes will drift or reintroduce the same ambiguity under new names.

Scope: publish the canonical owner map for execution lifecycle, zone and capability and provenance policy, evidence and receipts and checkpoints and revocation, FCPC framing, FCPS durable objects, store invariants, placement and leases, host orchestration, SDK authoring surfaces, and CLI and operator projections. Name forbidden overlaps explicitly so future work knows what not to reintroduce.

Acceptance: each major noun has a single owner, forbidden overlaps are listed, and later beads can use this as the contract for where new code belongs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:03.451196Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:26:02.337663Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:26:02.337375Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","map","mesh-first","ownership","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6huk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-2d0vg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:19.435907Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2062,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6huk","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-27: Published canonical owner map at docs/FCP3_Canonical_Owner_Map.md. Maps every major noun to a single owner crate. Defines 7 forbidden overlaps (F1-F7): health aggregation, rollout decisions, enforcement ordering, progress/cancellation schemas, readiness model, policy manipulation in CLI, credential store trait. Lists MOVE candidates: CancelReason, CleanupBehavior, ProgressUpdate, RolloutDecision/Evidence/Observation, EnforcementCheckOrder to fcp-core. Unblocks P1.3 (guardrails), P1.4 (acceptance contracts), P1.5 (scorecard), P2.1-P2.3 (crate carving).","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:26:01Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qc3d7","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-mesh: dispatch_gossip_payload parses unbounded JSON before gossip caps","description":"P2 explore-phase. Evidence: crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:1686-1693 calls serde_json::from_slice(payload) on attacker-controlled transport bytes before any payload-length check, then only applies summary/IBLT bounds after parse inside handle_gossip_message/handle_summary. Repro: call MeshNode::dispatch_gossip_payload with a JSON Summary payload whose iblt array is extremely large (for example tens of MB of integers); serde_json allocates/parses the entire body before max_iblt_bytes or max_peer_states can reject it. This makes the raw gossip transport entrypoint an unbounded allocation/CPU DoS surface even though later summary handlers are bounded. Suggested fix: reject payloads larger than a derived max wire budget before serde_json::from_slice (or switch to a bounded reader / canonical CBOR with explicit size caps), and keep the already-parsed dispatch_gossip_message path for trusted transport layers.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:19:11.534076Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:34:56.668298Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qc3d7","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-mesh: dispatch_gossip_payload parses unbounded JSON before gossip caps","description":"P2 explore-phase. Evidence: crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:1686-1693 calls serde_json::from_slice(payload) on attacker-controlled transport bytes before any payload-length check, then only applies summary/IBLT bounds after parse inside handle_gossip_message/handle_summary. Repro: call MeshNode::dispatch_gossip_payload with a JSON Summary payload whose iblt array is extremely large (for example tens of MB of integers); serde_json allocates/parses the entire body before max_iblt_bytes or max_peer_states can reject it. This makes the raw gossip transport entrypoint an unbounded allocation/CPU DoS surface even though later summary handlers are bounded. Suggested fix: reject payloads larger than a derived max wire budget before serde_json::from_slice (or switch to a bounded reader / canonical CBOR with explicit size caps), and keep the already-parsed dispatch_gossip_message path for trusted transport layers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:19:11.534076Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:41:45.910644Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:41:45.910360Z","close_reason":"Rejected oversized raw gossip JSON before deserialize and added a regression test for over-cap payloads","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qgu6t","title":"[CRITICAL] Fix non-production default URLs in DocuSign and Plaid connectors","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:12.695374Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:53.020397Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:53.020226Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","critical","hardening"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qgu6t","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1z643","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:19.245594Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1938,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qgu6t","author":"jemanuel","text":"CRITICAL: Non-Production Default URLs

Two connectors ship DEFAULT_BASE_URL constants pointing at non-production environments. Any deployment using the out-of-the-box defaults will silently hit sandbox/demo infrastructure rather than the provider's production API. This is the most dangerous class of hard-coded constant because it fails silently — requests succeed but against the wrong data.

FINDINGS:

1. DocuSign (connectors/docusign/src/client.rs:38):
   DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "https://demo.docusign.net/restapi/v2.1/accounts"
   - This is the DocuSign DEMO environment, not production
   - Production URLs follow the pattern: https://na1.docusign.net/..., https://eu.docusign.net/..., etc.
   - The connector's doctor output (connector.rs:1050) mentions both demo and production patterns, but the *default* is demo
   - Any user who doesn't explicitly override base_url gets demo

2. Plaid (connectors/plaid/src/client.rs:21):
   DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "https://sandbox.plaid.com"
   - This is the Plaid SANDBOX environment, not production or development
   - Plaid has three environments: sandbox, development, production
   - All data in sandbox is fake test data
   - The connector explicitly names environments (connector.rs:18,22,65) but defaults to sandbox

FIX APPROACH:
- Remove the hard-coded default entirely from both connectors
- Make base_url a required configuration field (no default = forces explicit choice)
- Add environment enum (Production/Sandbox/Demo) to the configure() handler
- Update doctor/self_check to warn if running against non-production
- Update manifest.toml to document the required configuration
- Add tests verifying that unconfigured connectors refuse to start rather than silently hitting demo/sandbox

WHY THIS MATTERS:
FCP's security model is zone-based with capability tokens. A connector that silently routes traffic to a demo server undermines the entire trust chain — real capability tokens are sent to a test environment, potentially leaking production intent to sandbox infrastructure. This is a category of bug that the FCP security invariants should mechanically prevent.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qj1i8","title":"Probe Issue","description":"Type: task","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:52:30.129189Z","updated_at":"2026-03-23T08:00:00.645155Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T08:00:00.645155Z","close_reason":"Probe task - not actionable.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qmy9x","title":"[MOR/C3.7] Encode zone binding as type-level invariant via ZoneBound wrapper","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:59.254533Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T23:20:35.209609Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T23:20:35.208909Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["security","type-safety","zone"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qmy9x","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8whb3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:08.986858Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qmy9x","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ky3yv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:10.523371Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2646,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qmy9x","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Create a ZoneBound<Z, T> wrapper type that binds a value T to a specific zone Z at the type level. Connector instances would be ZoneBound<Z, ConnectorInstance> — the zone is part of the type, preventing accidental cross-zone operations. WHY: A7 Type-Theoretic found zone boundary crossing is runtime-checked, not type-enforced. J1 Deontic found single-zone binding is documented only with no mechanical enforcement. A connector receiving requests from multiple zones would only be caught at runtime. IMPLEMENTATION SKETCH: ```rust pub struct ZoneBound<T> { inner: T, zone_id: ZoneId, } impl<T> ZoneBound<T> { pub fn bind(inner: T, zone: ZoneId) -> Self { Self { inner, zone_id: zone } } pub fn zone_id(&self) -> &ZoneId { &self.zone_id } // Operations can only access inner if zone matches pub fn with_zone_check<R>(&self, expected_zone: &ZoneId, f: impl FnOnce(&T) -> R) -> Result<R, ZoneViolation> { if &self.zone_id != expected_zone { return Err(ZoneViolation { ... }); } Ok(f(&self.inner)) } } ``` NOTE: Full phantom-type zone encoding (where zones are types, not values) requires const generics or associated types, which may be too complex for 150 connectors. The value-level ZoneBound wrapper with runtime check is a pragmatic middle ground that still enforces the binding at the API level. WHERE: crates/fcp-core/src/zone.rs or new module. Used in fcp-host when creating connector instances. ACCEPTANCE: (1) ZoneBound<T> type exists. (2) Connector instances wrapped in ZoneBound. (3) Operations require zone match check. (4) Cross-zone operation returns ZoneViolation error. (5) At least 4 tests: bind and access within zone, cross-zone access rejected, zone_id accessor, serialization. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 2-3 days. DEPENDS ON: C3.1 (phantom tokens) — the zone binding interacts with token zone_id validation.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:36:52Z"},{"id":2660,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qmy9x","author":"jemanuel","text":"C3.7 complete: (1) ZoneBound<T> type exists with bind(), zone_id(), with_zone_check(), with_zone_check_mut(), into_inner(). (2) Cross-zone access returns FcpError::ZoneViolation. (3) Serde roundtrip works. (4) Zone immutable after binding. (5) 8 acceptance tests. Commit ac6996dd.","created_at":"2026-04-10T23:20:34Z"}]}
